chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1300 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.7103/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.7104/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 20.05.2025 M/s Imran Ali Mithani & Mohsin Ali, advocates for applicant ------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Muhammad Rafiq Haslo son of Bakhshal alias Bux Ali seeks protective bail in FIR No.124/2025, registered at P.S. Kamaber City, for offence under sections 302, 337-H(ii), PPC. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicant is innocent and has been falsely implicated in this case due to mala fide intentions and ulterior motives otherwise he has nothing to do with the alleged offence; that no specific role has been assigned to the applicant/accused, he went to deceased house along with main accused for taking her hand and on this aspect he has been implicated in this case by complainant; that he is a law abiding citizen and intends to surrender before learned trial Court concerned but he apprehends his arrest at the hands of local police in order to humiliate and disgrace him, as such, he needs some protection to enable to appear before the trial Court concerned. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named for a period of seven days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith.

Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named for a period of seven days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of seven days from today or whenever applicant surrenders before the trial Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stands automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
